key_points: Public facilities are essential services provided mainly by the government to ensure the welfare and development of all citizens
These facilities include water supply, sanitation, electricity, healthcare, education, public transport and communication systems
Public facilities are necessary because they fulfill basic needs that are required for a healthy and productive life
The government collects taxes from citizens and uses this revenue to build and maintain public facilities
Equal access to public facilities is important to reduce social and economic inequalities in society
In some cases private companies may provide services, but the government must regulate them to ensure fairness and accessibility
Lack of proper public facilities can lead to poor health, limited education opportunities and social inequality
Efficient management of public facilities contributes to economic growth and national development
examples: Municipal corporations supply drinking water and maintain sanitation systems in cities
Government hospitals provide healthcare services especially for low-income citizens
Public transport systems such as buses, metros and trains help people travel for work and education
Electricity boards supply power for households, industries and public services
formulas: Public Facilities → Government responsibility + Public welfare + Infrastructure
Social development → Public services + Equal access + Effective governance
Public services funding → Taxes + Government planning + Administration
exam_tips: Explain the concept of public facilities with clear examples
Mention the role of government and taxation in providing public services
Discuss the importance of equal access to facilities in reducing inequality
Relate public facilities to economic development and social welfare
